reason: Second fix round on PR #40. The previous attempt left the /files file-tree bug unfixed: every folder rendered as empty because `buildtree` (in `src/dashboard/components/files/filetree.tsx`) never recursed — it set `children` to `null` whenever an entry's children had been loaded, and to `[]` otherwise, so a folder whose children were fetched was still rendered with `children: null` and its subtree never rendered. Fix: (1) extracted `buildtree` from the FileTree component into a top-level exported function that takes the state slices it needs (childrenMap, openPaths, loadingPaths, sortKey) and recurses on `entry.type === 'dir' && openpaths.has(entry.path)` with `childrenmap[entry.path] ?? []`; (2) dropped the `children = childrenmap[path] ?? entries` line at the top — the caller now threads whether it's the root (useQuery data) or a child (recursive call) explicitly; (3) changed the render guard in `treenoderow` to `node.children?.map(...)` so closed folders and files render nothing. Added a regression test in `tests/unit/file-tree.spec.ts` that calls `buildtree` on a sample childrenMap with `app` open and `app/page.tsx` + `app/api` populated, and asserts the returned tree contains nested `treenode`s for both under the `app` node (the exact case that broke the live preview). Verify gates (typecheck/lint/format/tests) all green after `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` (needed to pull `@monaco-editor/react`, which is in the PR's package.json but missing from node_modules on the worktree — pre-existing, not introduced by this round).
